seoTitle: How to Connect Microsoft Project to Power BI for Reporting
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Microsoft Project to Power BI so project timelines, tasks, and resources become visual, easy-to-share dashboards.
intro: Microsoft Project holds a lot of valuable planning data, but it isn't built for the kind of visual reporting that stakeholders want to see. This guide walks through connecting Microsoft Project to Power BI so project data can be turned into dashboards. It's aimed at project managers and analysts who need clearer, shareable views of progress and resource allocation.

steps.heading: Set up Microsoft Project
steps.body: Make sure your project file or Project Online instance has the data you want to report on organized and up to date before connecting it to Power BI.

steps.heading: Connect to Power BI
steps.body: In Power BI Desktop, use Get Data to find the Microsoft Project connector, then authenticate and select the project data you want to import.

steps.heading: Visualize the data
steps.body: Build charts and tables around key project fields like task status, timelines, and resource assignments to surface useful insights.

steps.heading: Apply tips and best practices
steps.body: Keep your Project file's task and resource naming consistent, since clean source data makes for much easier reporting in Power BI.

steps.heading: Refresh for up-to-date reporting
steps.body: Set up a refresh schedule so your Power BI dashboard reflects the latest changes made in Microsoft Project without manual re-importing.

faq.question: Do I need Project Online or does this work with desktop Project files?
faq.answer: Both are generally supported, though the exact connection method and available data can differ depending on whether you're using Project Online or a local file.

faq.question: Can Power BI show resource allocation from Microsoft Project?
faq.answer: Yes, resource assignment and workload data can be imported and visualized, which is useful for spotting over-allocated team members.

faq.question: How current is the data in Power BI after connecting?
faq.answer: The data is only as current as your last refresh, so scheduling regular refreshes keeps the dashboard aligned with the live project plan.

faq.question: Is this integration suitable for tracking multiple projects at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can import data from several project files or a Project Online instance to build a portfolio-level view across multiple projects.
